title: PlanktonSimulationDomain
description:
Spatial description of the VTK Image Data (vtkImageData) grid used as the plankton RDA simulation domain in FiniteDifferenceMethod4PDES.
Two domain types are supported:
1. **PDE spatial domain** (`domainType: spatial-pde`) — the physical space
   over which the reaction-diffusion-advection PDE is solved. Each grid cell
   holds phytoplankton (X1) and zooplankton (X2) values at one timestep.
   Geometry is a LineString (1D) or Polygon (2D).

2. **ODE phase-portrait domain** (`domainType: phase-portrait-ode`) — for
   pure ODE runs, the VTK grid maps state-space onto a 3D voxel grid via
   Room.getDGL(). The geometry is abstract (no real-world CRS).

VTK Image Data grid structure (from Room.java):
  j = x + y * dim[0] + z * dim[0] * dim[1]
  position[x,y,z] = origin + [x * spacing[0], y * spacing[1], z * spacing[2]]

VTI point-data array names follow the convention:
  "phase Phytoplankton", "phase Zooplankton"   (phase-space arrays)
  "local Phytoplankton", "local Zooplankton"   (local statistics)
  "global Phytoplankton", "global Zooplankton" (global statistics)

domainType.description: 'spatial-pde': real spatial domain of a PDE simulation. 'phase-portrait-ode': abstract state-space grid for ODE visualisation.

dimensions.description: Number of grid points in each direction (vtkImageData WholeExtent)

spacing.description: Grid cell spacing in each direction (VTK Spacing attribute)

arrayNames.description: Names of the VTK point-data arrays in the VTI files. For the plankton model: 'phase Phytoplankton', 'phase Zooplankton', 'local Phytoplankton', 'local Zooplankton', etc.

vtiSeries.description: References to the VTI time-series files.

stepCount.description: Total number of timestep files in the series

initFile.description: Path to the initial-condition VTI file (step = 1)

geometry.description: GeoJSON geometry of the simulation domain extent. LineString for 1D PDE, Polygon for 2D PDE, Point for ODE phase-portrait.

crs.description: Coordinate reference system. Use 'model-units' for abstract numerical domains. Use an EPSG URI for real geographic domains.
